page.content: In the analysis of coefficients (standard error), the table presents data for various tests across frequencies per 10 years. The frequency (per 10 year) row includes values of 2, 3, 5, 10, and 40. For the saliva swab test, the coefficients are 0.82(0.32)∞ at 2, 3.07(0.22)* at 3, 3.99(0.24)* at 5, 5.07(0.29)* at 10, and 5.32(0.27)* at 40. The video capsule test shows coefficients of 3.20(0.22)* at 2, 4.55(0.23)* at 3, 5.57(0.28)* at 5, 6.03(0.30)* at 10, and 5.18(0.29)* at 40. The endoscopy test presents coefficients of 3.56(0.24)* at 2, 4.89(0.28)* at 3, 5.86(0.30)* at 5, 5.89(0.30)* at 10, and 4.53(0.30)* at 40. Additionally, the source of loss table reveals annual costs, with congestion rent costing $8,360,000,000, line losses at $24,686,565,760, unused electricity at $149,622,024,900, power interruptions totaling $150,000,000,000, CO2 emissions costing $1,223,000,000,000, and the total cost in 2009 amounting to $1,555,668,590,660.
